skill.name: unreal-engine-developer
skill.description: Unreal Engine 5 development guidance for AI agents using MCP, Unreal Python, Blueprints, C++, editor automation, asset workflows, and level-building tasks.
skill.summary: Build and automate Unreal Engine 5 projects with AI-assisted editor scripting and MCP workflows.
skill.author: DammianMiller

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, Unreal API names, local addresses, documentation links, and screenshot references. The confirmed risk is legitimate but powerful editor automation: community MCP server setup can execute arbitrary Unreal Python, console commands, asset changes, screenshots, and file writes, so publication should include a warning.

medium_findings.title: Powerful MCP Remote Editor Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill documents MCP setup that enables Unreal Editor remote execution and lists tools that execute arbitrary Python, run console commands, create or delete objects, and take screenshots. This is legitimate for Unreal automation, but it gives an AI agent broad control over a project and editor session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tools explicitly include Python execution, console commands, object deletion, and screenshots. The context is legitimate Unreal development, so this is elevated capability risk rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Community Package Manager Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run community MCP servers through package managers such as npx and uv. These are expected setup steps, but they execute third-party code and should be reviewed or pinned before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command and arguments are shown directly in MCP client configuration. No malicious package is proven, but the skill depends on executing external community code.

medium_findings.title: Project File Writes and Asset Mutation
medium_findings.description: Example workflows import assets, save levels, save assets, and write exported level data to a supplied output path. This is normal editor automation, but it can overwrite project data if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly perform imports, saves, deletion of actors, and JSON file writes. The behavior is expected for a game development skill, but it carries data-loss risk.

low_findings.title: Backtick Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Most static external command hits are inline code names, fenced configuration examples, Unreal API references, or tables. They are not Ruby shell backtick execution and do not by themselves create command injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown tables or fenced code blocks, not executable Ruby syntax. The scanner appears to have treated Markdown backticks as shell backticks.

low_findings.title: Screenshot Findings Do Not Show Upload or Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The skill mentions viewport screenshots as a verification feature, but no evidence shows network upload, credential capture, or external transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines only name screenshot capture or verification. I found no upload endpoint or instruction to transmit captured images.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Unreal Term False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations correspond to Unreal development terms, tool names, Blueprint events, or markdown content. No MD5, SHA1, DES, or encryption implementation is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation for Unreal editor tools and Blueprint nodes. I did not find evidence of cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive hashing.

low_findings.title: Local Address Guidance Is Not Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The skill suggests local bind addresses for troubleshooting MCP connection issues. This is configuration guidance, not scanning or probing remote hosts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line appears inside a troubleshooting checklist and references loopback or wildcard bind behavior. It does not include port scanning or host discovery logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Arbitrary Unreal Python Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill advertises an MCP tool that can execute Python inside Unreal Editor. This enables broad editor automation and should be limited to trusted projects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool description explicitly says it executes Python code. This is a direct capability, although it is part of the intended workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Server Launch Through Package Managers
dangerous_patterns.description: The MCP configuration launches community server code with npx or uv. Users should verify source repositories and dependency versions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command configurations are concrete and would execute external code. The reviewed text does not prove malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Write Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The level export workflow writes JSON to a provided output path. This is useful but can overwrite files if path handling is careless.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write operation is explicit. Risk depends on how the user supplies output_path and where the script runs.

content.user_title: Build Unreal Engine Projects with AI Agents
content.value_statement: Unreal Engine teams need reliable ways to automate editor tasks without losing project context. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for MCP-driven Unreal workflows, Python scripting, Blueprints, assets, and level iteration.
actual_capabilities: Explains MCP setup options for controlling Unreal Editor through Python remote execution or a plugin-based server.
Provides Unreal Python patterns for actors, assets, levels, Blueprints, imports, saves, and batch edits.
Guides Blueprint class creation, component setup, node graph manipulation, and compilation workflows.
Documents level design automation for spawning actors, assigning materials, exporting level data, and checking results.
Includes troubleshooting references for MCP connections, Python script errors, Blueprint compilation, and asset paths.
limitations: Requires a working Unreal Engine installation and correctly configured editor plugins.
Community MCP servers must be installed and trusted before agent control is enabled.
Examples may need updates for project-specific asset names, paths, engine versions, and plugins.
The skill provides guidance only; it does not include a bundled Unreal project or compiled plugin.

use_cases.title: Automate Editor Tasks
use_cases.description: Batch rename assets, import textures, save levels, and export structured level data from Unreal Editor.

use_cases.title: Prototype Gameplay Systems
use_cases.description: Create actors, components, Blueprint classes, and simple gameplay setups with AI-assisted scripting.

use_cases.title: Build Test Scenes Quickly
use_cases.description: Generate layout grids, place meshes, assign materials, move cameras, and capture visual verification screenshots.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Unreal MCP
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure Unreal Engine 5 for MCP control. List the required editor plugins, project settings, client configuration, and a simple connection test.

prompt_templates.title: Automate Asset Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a safe Unreal Python plan to rename selected static meshes with a prefix, validate asset paths, save changed assets, and report failures.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Blueprint Actor
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through creating a Blueprint actor with a mesh component, camera component, exposed variables, and a compile-and-save verification step.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Playable Test Level
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an MCP-driven workflow to spawn a small test level, assign materials, add lighting, place a player start, save the level, and verify the result.

output_examples.input: Create a simple Unreal scene with repeated floor tiles and one directional light.
output: A step-by-step Unreal Python workflow for loading the mesh asset, spawning tiles in a grid, adding a light, saving the level, and checking the outliner.
A verification checklist covering script execution, actor counts, asset references, and level save status.

output_examples.input: Help debug why my AI-controlled Unreal MCP server cannot connect.
output: A focused troubleshooting sequence for plugin status, remote execution settings, bind address, editor restart, and stale Node.js processes.
A clear distinction between editor setup problems, client configuration problems, and project plugin build problems.

output_examples.input: Prepare a Blueprint automation task for a new pickup actor.
output_examples.output: A production-oriented plan that creates the Blueprint class, adds components, sets defaults, compiles it, spawns an instance, saves assets, and validates behavior.

best_practices: Use source control before running scripts that create, delete, rename, or save Unreal assets.
Start with read-only MCP checks before enabling Python execution or project mutation.
Pin and review community MCP server dependencies before adding them to an AI client configuration.
anti_patterns: Running arbitrary generated Python against a production Unreal project without a backup or diff review.
Leaving remote execution enabled on a broad bind address after the automation task is complete.
Using placeholder asset paths from examples without validating that they match the project structure.

faq.question: Does this skill include an Unreal plugin?
faq.answer: No. It documents workflows and setup options for existing Unreal MCP servers and Unreal Python tooling.

faq.question: Can it modify my Unreal project?
faq.answer: Yes, if connected to an MCP server with editor execution enabled. Use source control and review generated actions.

faq.question: Which Unreal versions are referenced?
faq.answer: The skill references Unreal Engine 5.4 or later for one MCP option and Unreal Engine 5.5 or later for another.

faq.question: Does it support Blueprints?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Blueprint creation, components, event nodes, variables, node graph work, compilation, and spawning.

faq.question: Is network access required?
faq.answer: Initial setup may use package managers and GitHub resources. Local MCP communication also depends on editor connection settings.

faq.question: What is the main safety concern?
faq.answer: The main concern is broad editor control through Python execution, console commands, and project file writes.
